name: Install Homebrew
description: Installs Homebrew on macOS and Linux. Handles Xcode CLT (macOS) / build-tool dependencies (Linux), installs via the official installer, and exports brew shellenv to the appropriate shell startup files.

install_user.description:
User to own the Homebrew installation.
On macOS root is not permitted; defaults to SUDO_USER if set, otherwise the first non-system user.
On Linux root installs are allowed; defaults to the current user.
Ignored when not running as root.

prefix.description:
Override the Homebrew installation prefix (HOMEBREW_PREFIX).
Defaults are /opt/homebrew (Apple Silicon), /usr/local (Intel macOS), /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew (Linux).
Leave empty to accept the platform default.

if_exists.description:
What to do when Homebrew is already installed at the resolved prefix.
'skip'      — continue to post-install steps without re-running the installer (default).
'fail'      — print an error and exit non-zero.
'reinstall' — uninstall then install fresh.

update.description: Run 'brew update' after installation to fetch the latest formula index.

export_path.description:
Controls which shell startup files receive 'eval "$(brew shellenv)"'.
'auto' writes to all relevant system-wide files (root + Linux) or user-scoped files (macOS / non-root).
'' (empty) skips all shell-env writes.
Newline-separated list of absolute paths: writes only to those files.

users.description:
Comma-separated list of additional users whose shell startup files should receive 'eval "$(brew shellenv)"'.
Only applies when export_path is 'auto' or a path list.

brew_git_remote.description:
Override HOMEBREW_BREW_GIT_REMOTE — the git remote for the Homebrew/brew repository.
Useful for air-gapped environments or mirrors.

core_git_remote.description:
Override HOMEBREW_CORE_GIT_REMOTE — the git remote for the homebrew-core tap.
Useful for air-gapped environments or mirrors.

no_install_from_api.description:
Set HOMEBREW_NO_INSTALL_FROM_API=1 during installation.
Forces homebrew-core to be cloned as a full git repository rather than relying on the JSON API.
Slower but useful in environments where the API endpoint is blocked.

logfile.description: Append install log to this file path.
